heading: Advanced Research Projects Agency–Infrastructure

children.heading: .—ARPA–I
children.label: 1 .—ARPA–I
children.text: The term “ARPA–I” means the Advanced Research Projects Agency–Infrastructure established by subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Department
children.label: 2 .—Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Director
children.label: 3 .—Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of ARPA–I appointed under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: a unit of State or local government;

children.label: B
children.text: an institution of higher education;

children.label: C
children.text: a commercial entity;

children.label: D
children.text: a research foundation;

children.label: E
children.text: a trade or industry research collaborative;

children.label: F
children.text: a federally funded research and development center;

children.label: G
children.text: a research facility owned or funded by the Department;

children.label: H
children.text: a collaborative that includes relevant international entities; and

children.label: I
children.text: a consortia of 2 or more entities described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (H).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term “infrastructure” means any transportation method or facility that facilitates the transit of goods or people within the United States (including territories).

children.label: i
children.text: roads;

children.label: ii
children.text: highways;

children.label: iii
children.text: bridges;

children.label: iv
children.text: airports;

children.label: v
children.text: rail lines;

children.label: vi
children.text: harbors; and

children.label: vii
children.text: pipelines.

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 6 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Transportation.
to overcome long-term challenges; and
to advance the state of the art for United States transportation infrastructure.

children.label: i
children.text: lower the long-term costs of infrastructure development, including costs of planning, construction, and maintenance;

children.label: ii
children.text: reduce the lifecycle impacts of transportation infrastructure on the environment, including through the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions;

children.label: iii
children.text: contribute significantly to improving the safe, secure, and efficient movement of goods and people; and

children.label: iv
children.text: promote the resilience of infrastructure from physical and cyber threats; and

children.label: B
children.text: to ensure that the United States is a global leader in developing and deploying advanced transportation infrastructure technologies and materials.

children.label: A
children.text: advance novel, early-stage research with practicable application to transportation infrastructure;

children.label: B
children.text: translate techniques, processes, and technologies, from the conceptual phase to prototype, testing, or demonstration;

children.label: C
children.text: develop advanced manufacturing processes and technologies for the domestic manufacturing of novel transportation-related technologies; and

children.label: D
children.text: accelerate transformational technological advances in areas in which industry entities are unlikely to carry out projects due to technical and financial uncertainty.

children.heading: .—Appointment
children.label: 1 .—Appointment
children.text: ARPA–I shall be headed by a Director, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.heading: .—Qualifications
children.label: 2 .—Qualifications
children.text: The Director shall be an individual who, by reason of professional background and experience, is especially qualified to advise the Secretary regarding, and manage research programs addressing, matters relating to the development of science and technology solutions to advance United States transportation infrastructure.

children.label: A
children.text: be located within the Office of the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology; and

children.label: B
children.text: report to the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Relationship to other programs
children.label: 4 .—Relationship to other programs
children.text: No other program within the Department shall report to the Director.

children.label: A
children.text: approving new programs within ARPA–I;

children.label: B
children.text: developing funding criteria, and assessing the success of programs, to achieve the goals described in subsection (c)(1) through the establishment of technical milestones;

children.label: C
children.text: administering available funding by providing to eligible entities assistance to achieve the goals described in subsection (c)(1);

children.label: D
children.text: terminating programs carried out under this section that are not achieving the goals of the programs; and

children.label: E
children.text: establishing a process through which eligible entities can submit to ARPA–I unsolicited research proposals for assistance under this section in accordance with subsection (f).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Director shall establish and maintain within ARPA–I a staff with sufficient qualifications and expertise to enable ARPA–I to carry out the responsibilities under this section, in conjunction with other operations of the Department.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Director shall designate employees to serve as program directors for ARPA–I.

children.label: i
children.text: establishing research and development goals for the applicable program, including by convening workshops and conferring with outside experts;

children.label: ii
children.text: publicizing the goals of the applicable program;

children.label: iii
children.text: soliciting applications for specific areas of particular promise, especially in areas that the private sector or the Federal Government are not likely to carry out absent assistance from ARPA–I;

children.label: iv
children.text: establishing research collaborations for carrying out the applicable program;

children.label: I
children.text: the novelty and scientific and technical merit of proposed projects;

children.label: II
children.text: the demonstrated capabilities of eligible entities to successfully carry out proposed projects;

children.label: III
children.text: the extent to which an eligible entity took into consideration future commercial applications of a proposed project, including the feasibility of partnering with 1 or more commercial entities; and

children.label: IV
children.text: such other criteria as the Director may establish;

children.label: vi
children.text: identifying innovative cost-sharing arrangements for projects carried out or funded by ARPA–I;

children.label: vii
children.text: monitoring the progress of projects supported under the applicable program;

children.label: viii
children.text: identifying mechanisms for commercial application of successful technology development projects, including through establishment of partnerships between eligible entities and commercial entities; and

children.label: I
children.text: program restructuring; or

children.label: II
children.text: termination of applicable research partnerships or projects.

children.label: i
children.text: shall serve for a term of 3 years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may be reappointed for any subsequent term of service.

children.label: i
children.text: make appointments of scientific, engineering, and professional personnel, without regard to the civil service laws;

children.label: ii
children.text: fix the basic pay of such personnel at such rate as the Director may determine, but not to exceed level II of the Executive Schedule, without regard to the civil service laws; and

children.label: II
children.text: an amount equal to 25 percent of the annual rate of basic pay of the employee; and

children.label: III
children.text: the amount of the applicable limitation for a calendar year under .section 5307(a)(1) of title 5

children.heading: .—Private recruiting firms
children.label: B .—Private recruiting firms
children.text: The Director may enter into a contract with a private recruiting firm for the hiring of qualified technical staff to carry out this section.

children.heading: .—Additional staff
children.label: C .—Additional staff
children.text: The Director may use all authorities available to the Secretary to hire administrative, financial, and clerical staff, as the Director determines to be necessary to carry out this section.

children.text: each such project that examines topics or technologies closely related to other activities funded by the Department, including an analysis of whether the Director achieved compliance with subsection (i)(1) in supporting the project; and

children.heading: .—Strategic vision roadmap
children.label: 2 .—Strategic vision roadmap
children.text: Not later than , and not less frequently than once every 4 years thereafter, the Director shall submit to the relevant authorizing and appropriations committees of Congress a roadmap describing the strategic vision that ARPA–I will use to guide the selection of future projects for technology investment during the 4 fiscal-year period beginning on the date of submission of the report.October 1, 2022

children.heading: .—Federal Demonstration of Technologies
children.label: j .—Federal Demonstration of Technologies
children.text: The Director shall seek opportunities to partner with purchasing and procurement programs of Federal agencies to demonstrate technologies resulting from activities funded through ARPA–I.
to accomplish the goals of ARPA–I;
to develop institutional capacity in advanced transportation infrastructure technologies and materials;
to engage underserved populations in developing, demonstrating, and deploying those technologies and materials; and
to otherwise address the needs of ARPA–I.
partner with university transportation centers under section 5505 to accomplish the goals, and address the needs, of ARPA–I; and

sponsor and select for funding, in accordance with section 5505, competitively selected university transportation center grants, in addition to the assistance provided under section 5505, to address targeted technology and material goals of ARPA–I.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary may enter into an arrangement with the National Academy of Sciences under which the National Academy shall conduct an evaluation of the achievement by ARPA–I of the goals described in subsection (c)(1).December 27, 2024

children.label: A

children.label: A

children.label: B
children.text: information relating to investments provided to an eligible entity from a third party (such as a venture capital firm, a hedge fund, and a private equity firm), including any percentage of ownership of an eligible entity provided in return for such an investment;

shall be in addition to any existing authority provided to the Secretary; and

children.heading: .—Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 .—Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: .—Budget request
children.label: A .—Budget request
children.text: The budget request for ARPA–I shall be separate from the budget request of the remainder of the Department.

children.heading: .—Appropriations
children.label: B .—Appropriations
children.text: The funding appropriated for ARPA–I shall be separate and distinct from the funding appropriated for the remainder of the Department.

children.label: i

children.label: B
children.text: none may be used for the construction of any new building or facility during the 5-year period beginning on the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Investment Act of 2021.
